initial_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(R)   :- R = 0..N-1.
col(C)   :- C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  First column with value 8 in each row
first8col(R,F) :- row(R), F = #min{C : input(R,C,8)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells left and right of the first 8 (grounded by col(C))
before_first8col(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), first8col(R,F), C < F.
after_first8col(R,C)  :- row(R), col(C), first8col(R,F), C > F.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  First nonzero cell after the first 8 (excluding 0 and 8)
%  This yields the colour X and the column C of that cell.
first_nonzero(R,C,X) :-
    row(R), first8col(R,F), col(C),
    C = #min{C2 : input(R,C2,V), V != 0, V != 8, C2 > F},
    input(R,C,X).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation mapping 36, 42, 24
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 3, Y = 6.
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 4, Y = 2.
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 2, Y = 4.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour of the row that will replace zeros left of the first 8
row_X_Y(R,Y) :- row(R), first_nonzero(R,C,X), transform(R,Y,X).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output rules  exactly one colour per output cell

% 8 stays 8
output(R,C,8) :- input(R,C,8), row(R).

% zeros left of the first 8 become the transformed colour Y_R
output(R,C,Y) :- input(R,C,0), before_first8col(R,C), row_X_Y(R,Y).

% zeros right of the first 8 but still before the X cell become Y_R
output(R,C,Y) :- input(R,C,0), after_first8col(R,C), C < C1,
                  first_nonzero(R,C1,X), row_X_Y(R,Y).

% zeros right of the X cell stay 0
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), after_first8col(R,C), C > C1.

% the X cell itself keeps its colour
output(R,C,X) :- input(R,C,X), C = C1.

% other cells keep their original colour (except the special ones)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), row(R),
    after_first8col(R,C),
    Col != 0, Col != 8,
    Col != X, Col != Y,
    Col != X1, Col != X2.

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Consistency: at most one X_R per row
:- first_nonzero(R,C1,X1), first_nonzero(R,C2,X2), X1 != X2, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(R)   :- R = 0..N-1.
col(C)   :- C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  First column with value 8 in each row
first8col(R,F) :- row(R), F = #min{C : input(R,C,8)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells left and right of the first 8 (grounded by col(C))
before_first8col(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), first8col(R,F), C < F.
after_first8col(R,C)  :- row(R), col(C), first8col(R,F), C > F.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  First nonzero cell after the first 8 (excluding 0 and 8)
%  This yields the colour X and the column C of that cell.
first_nonzero(R,C,X) :-
    row(R), first8col(R,F), col(C),
    C = #min{C2 : input(R,C2,V), V != 0, V != 8, C2 > F},
    input(R,C,X).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation mapping 36, 42, 24
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 3, Y = 6.
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 4, Y = 2.
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 2, Y = 4.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour of the row that will replace zeros left of the first 8
row_X_Y(R,Y) :- row(R), first_nonzero(R,C,X), transform(R,Y,X).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output rules  exactly one colour per output cell

% 8 stays 8
output(R,C,8) :- input(R,C,8), row(R).

% zeros left of the first 8 become the transformed colour Y_R
output(R,C,Y) :- input(R,C,0), before_first8col(R,C), row_X_Y(R,Y).

% zeros right of the first 8 but still before the X cell become Y_R
output(R,C,Y) :- input(R,C,0), after_first8col(R,C), C < C1,
                  first_nonzero(R,C1,X), row_X_Y(R,Y).

% zeros right of the X cell stay 0
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), after_first8col(R,C), C > C1.

% the X cell itself keeps its colour
output(R,C,X) :- input(R,C,X), C = C1.

% other cells keep their original colour (except the special ones)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), row(R),
    after_first8col(R,C),
    Col != 0, Col != 8,
    Col != X, Col != Y,
    Col != X1, Col != X2.

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Consistency: at most one X_R per row
:- first_nonzero(R,C1,X1), first_nonzero(R,C2,X2), X1 != X2, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.

final_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(R)   :- R = 0..N-1.
col(C)   :- C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  First column with value 8 in each row
first8col(R,F) :- row(R), F = #min{C : input(R,C,8)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells left and right of the first 8 (grounded by col(C))
before_first8col(R,C) :- row(R), col(C), first8col(R,F), C < F.
after_first8col(R,C)  :- row(R), col(C), first8col(R,F), C > F.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  First nonzero cell after the first 8 (excluding 0 and 8)
%  This yields the colour X and the column C of that cell.
first_nonzero(R,C,X) :-
    row(R), first8col(R,F), col(C),
    C = #min{C2 : input(R,C2,V), V != 0, V != 8, C2 > F},
    input(R,C,X).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation mapping 36, 42, 24
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 3, Y = 6.
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 4, Y = 2.
transform(R,Y,X) :- X = 2, Y = 4.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour of the row that will replace zeros left of the first 8
row_X_Y(R,Y) :- row(R), first_nonzero(R,C,X), transform(R,Y,X).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output rules  exactly one colour per output cell

% 8 stays 8
output(R,C,8) :- input(R,C,8), row(R).

% zeros left of the first 8 become the transformed colour Y_R
output(R,C,Y) :- input(R,C,0), before_first8col(R,C), row_X_Y(R,Y).

% zeros right of the first 8 but still before the X cell become Y_R
output(R,C,Y) :- input(R,C,0), after_first8col(R,C), C < C1,
                  first_nonzero(R,C1,X), row_X_Y(R,Y).

% zeros right of the X cell stay 0
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), after_first8col(R,C), C > C1.

% the X cell itself keeps its colour
output(R,C,X) :- input(R,C,X), C = C1.

% other cells keep their original colour (except the special ones)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), row(R),
    after_first8col(R,C),
    Col != 0, Col != 8,
    Col != X, Col != Y,
    Col != X1, Col != X2.

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Consistency: at most one X_R per row
:- first_nonzero(R,C1,X1), first_nonzero(R,C2,X2), X1 != X2, row(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.
